reasoning: Without the Ukraine transit route (which the prompt explicitly states ended in January 2025), Russian gas to the EU is geographically and mathematically limited to the remaining European strand of the TurkStream pipeline (~3.9 bcm/quarter maximum capacity) and LNG deliveries (historically ~4-5 bcm/quarter). The maximum plausible combined volume maxes out around 9-10 bcm per quarter. Reaching 15 bcm would require a physical impossibility in utilization rates of existing infrastructure and a massive sudden increase in Russian LNG production capacity and European off-take, ignoring EU's phase-out trajectories outright. Thus, the possibility of hitting 15 bcm in any single quarter of 2025 under these constraints is exceedingly remote.
